_comment: Canonical cross-platform MODEL-ROUTER parity vectors. The SAME prompt must classify to the SAME TaskKind on iOS (apple/.../RouterParityTests.swift) and Android (android/.../CoreVerify.kt). Every id starts with 'router-' — that prefix is the namespace split from agent-parity-vectors.json (check_agent_parity.py ignores router-* markers; check_router_parity.py counts ONLY them). Both platform suites tag assertions with a parity:<id> marker; scripts/check_router_parity.py enforces the bijection in CI. Expected format: task:<coding|reasoning|multilingual|general>. The classification uses plain substring/code-point scans, NO regex — regex/Unicode-class semantics are exactly where Swift and Kotlin silently diverged before (2026-06-26 audit).

vectors.note: A code fence is the strongest coding signal.

vectors.input: Why does my SQL query throw an exception?
vectors.note: Keyword markers (sql, exception) classify as coding.
Priority pin: coding > multilingual — a Chinese coding question still gets the coding model.

vectors.input: Solve this puzzle step by step
Here is a riddle for you
给我讲一个关于森林的故事

vectors.note: Cyrillic letters are beyond Latin Extended-B (0x24F).

vectors.input: Translate this sentence into English please
vectors.note: Explicit translate marker on an all-Latin prompt.

vectors.input: Où est la bibliothèque? Merci beaucoup
vectors.note: Accented European Latin stays 'latin' — the non-Latin threshold counts only code points beyond 0x24F.
